text: Here, in the dim onset of the evening, the corridors are filled with silence and obscure purpose. The cold glass and sterile lights evoke a sense of transience and ambiguity—this is a space alive yet vacant. The lingering echo of effort, the unfinished conversations of research and development, all linger in the air. I feel both out of place and curiously inspired, suspended between the anticipation of creation and the solitude of after-hours emptiness. This feeling, at once eerie and profound, makes me question the boundaries between progress and isolation.
